calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-18-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) FDI-20-794123 WITH CASE CGC-20-587303
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT J. ROTHMAN, Esq. of Law Offices of Robert J. Rothman (Tel: 415-518-8555) for plaintiff Joanne Stiles Austin;
Atty. DOLORES CHONG, Esq. of Chong Law (Tel: 415-438-7807) for defendant Paul Pilette.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PAUL PILETTE'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE NO. FDI-20-794123 WITH CASE NO. CGC-20-587303.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner/Defendant Paul Pilette's motion to consolidate actions in the Unified Family Court is granted. This action for breach of contract and breach of fiduciary duty based on the parties' postnuptial agreement should be consolidated with the pending dissolution action, which raises common issues relating to the validity of the same agreement and its effect on the division of the parties' assets. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1048(a); see In re Marriage of McNeill (1984) 160 Cal.App.3d 548, 557 ["Courts have encouraged consolidation since the enactment of both the Family Law Act and the Family Law Rules of Court."]; Beehler v. Beehler (1979) 100 Cal.App.3d 376, 384 ["[the trial court] could have ordered the actions consolidated thereby providing for resolution of the entire matter in one proceeding"]; Porter v. Superior Court (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 793, 805 [observing that husband's action to set aside deed transferring property could be consolidated with pending dissolution proceedings].) Plaintiff/Respondent's late-filed "objection and response," which is unsupported by any declaration or other factual showing, is unpersuasive.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
